stocks.recent_news: Strong order momentum in past 3-4 days: May 5, 2026 - secured new work orders totalling Rs 176.28 cr from Government of Odisha (Kalinga Stadium upgradation Rs 29.48 cr), Canara Bank (Rs 61.81 cr office + residential Kochi), Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (Rs 50.98 cr Phase-B Odisha), IIM Sambalpur (Rs 29.24 cr), and APEDA. May 7, 2026 - appointed Project Management Agency by Power Finance Corporation for CSR projects worth ~Rs 103.47 cr across multiple states. Combined ~Rs 280 cr new orders in 3 days. Apr 11, 2026 signed two MoUs with HUDCO (August Kranti Bhawan redevelopment + project funding). Consolidated order book ~Rs 1.3 lakh cr. Stock at Rs 101.23 on May 8 (NSE), +9.31% w/w, +15% m/m. 52w range Rs 77.18 - Rs 130.70. Market cap ~Rs 26,150 cr. Q3 FY26 PAT +38.5% YoY (+25.9% QoQ); FY26 full-year revenue Rs 12,273 cr, PAT Rs 557.47 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Government infrastructure / construction PSUs benefiting from sustained capex push - housing, redevelopment, institutional construction pipelines remain healthy. NBCC operates asset-light Project Management Consultant model. Real estate / construction sentiment supportive in early May 2026 with steady government order flow.
stocks.peer_comparison: PSU construction/redevelopment specialist with few direct listed peers. Order-book-to-revenue ratio of ~10x is sector-leading. Recent stock momentum (+15% m/m) outpaces broader infra indices. Q3 PAT growth (+38.5% YoY) ahead of larger EPC peers.
red_flags: Stock has rallied 15% in past month - near-term technical extension after big move
Execution pace historically lagged order book accretion - revenue conversion remains the watch
PSU dependency on government decision cycles creates lumpy revenue recognition risk
Still ~22% below 52w high of Rs 130.70 - prior unwind suggests rerating not complete
March 2026 SEBI filing showed 6.62 lakh promoter shares encumbered (small but disclosed)
catalysts: Continued order flow momentum (~Rs 280 cr added in 3 days)
Q4 FY26 / full-year results expected on or around June 1, 2026
HUDCO MoU monetisation pipeline conversion
Order book Rs 1.3 lakh cr provides multi-year visibility

stocks.recent_news: Most recent reported quarter Q3 FY26 (Dec): PAT +11.4% YoY to Rs 164.03 cr, sales +35.23% to Rs 1,598.62 cr; PBT +12.37% to Rs 216.39 cr. Segment mix mixed - Electrical Cables Rs 1,400.44 cr (+44% YoY), Communication Cables Rs 122.76 cr (-5.75% YoY), Copper Rods Rs 678.20 cr (+50.37%). Company is expanding fiber draw capacity to meet fiber-optic demand and is deepening Northeast India distribution on regional infra build-out. Stock at Rs 893.05 - well below 52w high Rs 1,700, near 52w low Rs 780. EBIT margins reportedly under pressure from distribution model changes and competition.
stocks.sector_context: Wires & cables sector in strong uptrend - Polycab posted record FY26 with revenue Rs 28,884 cr (+29% YoY) and PAT highest-ever; KEI also at new highs. Sector rallied up to 34% in past month on infra/T&D capex tailwinds. Sector backdrop is unambiguously positive.
stocks.peer_comparison: Lagging peers materially: ROE 13.58% vs Polycab 20.31%, KEI 16.83%, RR Kabel 17.07%. Stock at Rs 893 sits much closer to 52w low (Rs 780) than 52w high (Rs 1,700) while peers print all-time highs - sharp relative underperformance. Q3 sales growth (+35%) decent in absolute terms but communication-cables drag and margin compression set FINCABLES apart from sector leaders.
red_flags: Severe peer underperformance despite strong sector tailwinds
Communication cables segment declining YoY (-5.75%)
EBIT margin pressure cited from distribution model changes and increased competition
Stock ~47% below 52w high while peers print all-time highs
ROE materially below peer median in a capex-intensive sector
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results pending (typical mid-May timing)
Fiber capacity expansion ramp
Northeast India distribution push monetisation

stocks.recent_news: Board of Directors meeting scheduled May 14, 2026 to approve audited FY26 results and consider final dividend; Q4 FY26 analyst conference call May 15 at 4 PM IST. Trading window closed until May 16 per insider-trading regulations - typical pre-results blackout. Q3 FY26 PAT had risen 39.52% YoY to Rs 470.52 cr. Stock around Rs 448 in recent trading.
stocks.sector_context: Indian tyre market growing ~7.49% CAGR (2026-2034). Auto sector seeing demand-led recovery in FY26 - 2W retail +13.4%, premium motorcycles +40.7%, scooters +18.5%, electric 2W +21.8%. Commercial vehicles in goods-led upcycle ~+12.6% YoY supportive of replacement and OE tyre demand. Raw-material (rubber, crude derivatives) backdrop benign through Q4.
stocks.peer_comparison: Sector read-across positive ahead of Apollo's print: MRF Q4 FY26 PAT +37% YoY to Rs 680 cr with Rs 229 final dividend - confirms tyre-maker margin tailwinds. Apollo's Q3 PAT growth (+39.5%) was already ahead of MRF's quarterly trajectory; FY26 print likely to extend momentum.
red_flags: Trading window closure is procedural, not a flag
No company-specific governance or regulatory issues identified
Stock has not run up materially into the print - leaves room for upside but also signals lukewarm pre-event positioning
catalysts: Q4 FY26 earnings on May 14, 2026
Final dividend announcement May 14
Analyst/investor conference call May 15 (FY27 outlook)
Continued CV cycle and replacement-demand tailwind

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 7, 2026: Consolidated PAT Rs 233.45 cr (-17% YoY) due to one-time exceptional charge from Labour Codes implementation (higher gratuity/leave liabilities); revenue Rs 4,528 cr (+17.5% YoY). FY26 full-year revenue Rs 16,811 cr (+11.7%), PAT Rs 1,079.6 cr (+14.7%). Final dividend Rs 6.50/share (325% on Rs 2 face value). Defence segment Q4 revenue Rs 416 cr (+46.4% YoY). On Apr 30, 2026 announced acquisition of 30% stake in FEPL for Rs 129.6 cr with option to scale to 50%+1. Management guided 25% revenue growth for India ops in FY27; defence projected +30-40% to exceed Rs 3,000 cr in FY27 from Rs 11,000 cr defence order book; ATAGS production set for late 2026 / H2 FY27. Stock at Rs 2,007.90 on May 8, +19.24% in last month, near 52w high Rs 2,026.60.
stocks.sector_context: Auto components and CV space supported by global truck demand patterns and India CV cycle recovery; however US Class 8 truck orders dropped from ~45,000 in Feb 2026 to 17,600 in May 2026 - meaningful cyclical headwind. Indian defence sector benefits from government indigenisation push - ATAGS, ammunition, artillery contracts ramping. Aerospace sourcing diversifying away from China. India manufacturing themes continue to attract premium multiples.
stocks.peer_comparison: Most diversified large-cap Indian forging/components player with defence, aerospace, oil & gas exposure beyond auto - earnings base broader than pure peers like Sundram Fasteners, Motherson Sumi. Defence pipeline (Rs 11,000 cr order book) is unique for the auto-component cluster. Morgan Stanley OW with TP Rs 1,978 - now below current price after the +19% rally, leaving limited upside on consensus.
red_flags: Q4 PAT fell 17% YoY - though Labour Codes exceptional is a one-off, headline print is negative
FY27 guidance (25% growth) is aggressive and macro/tariff sensitive
US Class 8 truck order collapse (-61% Feb-to-May) is meaningful for Bharat Forge's North American CV exposure
Stock has rallied +19% into a weak-headline print - profit-booking risk near 52w high
ATAGS execution timeline (H2 FY27) carries program-management risk
Premium valuation vs auto-component peers leaves limited room if guidance slips
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 6.50/share - record date forthcoming
Defence revenue acceleration: +30-40% guided FY27 to exceed Rs 3,000 cr
ATAGS production commencement H2 FY27 (high incremental margin)
FEPL stake acquisition synergies (option to scale to 50%+1)
Labour Codes hit is one-time - margin should normalise into Q1 FY27

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 6, 2026: Standalone PAT Rs 2,746 cr (+34% YoY) - record quarterly profit; revenue Rs 16,006 cr (+31.8% YoY); EBITDA Rs 3,323 cr (+35.6% YoY) with margin 20.8%. FY26 full-year revenue Rs 58,732 cr (+17% YoY), PAT Rs 9,825 cr (+21% YoY), EBITDA margin 20.5% (+30 bps). Board declared final dividend Rs 150/share (1500%, record date May 29, 2026) AND approved tender-route buyback of up to 4,694,000 shares (1.68% of equity) at Rs 12,000/share, aggregating Rs 5,633 cr - combined dividend + buyback ~100% of FY26 PAT. April 2026 total sales +40% YoY (driven by exports); Chetak EV cumulative 727,779 units, EV scooter market share 20% (up from 11% in FY24). Stock at Rs 10,315.50 in early-May trading.
stocks.sector_context: Indian two-wheeler retail sales +13% YoY in April 2026 to ~19.16 lakh units; FY26 retail +13.4%, premium motorcycles +40.7%, scooters +18.5%. Sector sees rural recovery + EV inflection. Hero MotoCorp dispatches +85% YoY in April, TVS +7% YoY. Bajaj/TVS/Hero collectively control ~60% of E2W market. Export-led growth particularly strong for Bajaj given Africa / LatAm exposure.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q4 PAT growth (+34%) ahead of Hero, TVS pace; revenue growth (+32%) sector-leading. EV market share 20% (up from 11% in FY24) - rapid catch-up to TVS. Buyback at Rs 12,000/share is ~16% premium to CMP, signalling management confidence. Total payout 100% of FY26 PAT vs typical sector payout of 50-70%.
red_flags: Stock has rallied substantially - buyback floor (Rs 12,000) creates short-term cap on upside vs immediate gain implied for tendering shareholders
Domestic two-wheeler EV competitive intensity high with TVS leadership; Bajaj playing catch-up though gaining share
Heavy export exposure makes earnings sensitive to USD/INR and emerging market demand
Post buyback record date, stock could see technical de-rating if acceptance ratio low
catalysts: Buyback at Rs 12,000/share (1.68% of equity, Rs 5,633 cr) - tender process drives technical demand
Final dividend Rs 150/share record date May 29, 2026
Continued April-style 40% sales momentum into Q1 FY27
Chetak EV market share gains compounding into FY27
